sanskrit: अपि चेल्लोक्राज्यस्य हेतोः किं नु महीकृते। निहत्य धार्तराष्ट्रान्नः का प्रीति: स्याज्जनार्दन॥
transliteration: api ced trailokya-rājyasya hetoḥ kiṃ nu mahī-kṛte, nihatya dhārtarāṣṭrān naḥ kā prītiḥ syāj janārdana
literal_meaning: Even for the sake of the three-world kingdom, what to speak of the earth, having killed the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, what joy would there be for us, O Janārdana?
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s mind collapses into existential futility: even absolute sovereignty over all three worlds feels meaningless if it requires annihilating his own relational world.
psychological_state: Acute moral injury with nihilistic shutdown—pleasure pathways are offline because the relational substrate that gives reward its savor is perceived as already destroyed.
trigger_scenario: A high-achieving professional offered the promotion of a lifetime realizes the role requires dismantling the team that raised her; she freezes and asks, 'If I lose the people who make success matter, what’s the point?'
anchor_text: Victory is ash if it costs your soul.
